Class II chemical preservatives can only be directly added to fillings and toppings in kuehs, within regulatory limits.

changes.parameter: Class II chemical preservatives (e.g., sorbic acid, benzoic acid)
changes.notes: Class II chemical preservatives should not be directly added during the manufacturing of kueh.
Food additives must undergo safety assessment by SFA before use in food sold in Singapore.
Import or sale of food products containing unpermitted additives is prohibited.
All food businesses must comply with regulatory limits on contaminants.
Contaminants in food sold in Singapore must comply with SFA's regulatory limits.
Import or sale of food products containing contaminants exceeding regulatory limits is prohibited.
Import or sale of meat and food derived from meat containing these specific veterinary drug residues is prohibited under Regulation 33 of the Food Regulations.
All ingredients and additives used in prepacked food products must be declared in the list of ingredients on food labels.

changes.newLimit: Verification required via GSFA Online Database (Codex STAN 192-1995)
changes.notes: The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA) sets forth the conditions for permitted food additives in all foods. Users are encouraged to consult the database and its Preamble for specific provisions.
Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

changes.newLimit: Verification required via General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995)
changes.notes: Codex establishes maximum levels (MLs) and guideline levels for contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants in food and feed. These levels are legally permitted concentrations.
